MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Konstantine Demiris for and with Elaine Jennings, Petitioner.
Jennifer McGuire for Michael Linares, Respondent.

Mr. Demiris estimates trial at 8-10 court days, request for review hearing in 90 days for status in regards to discovery and trial setting purposes. Ms. McGuire stated she is ready for trial in 6 months. The Court requested an updated status report.

The 1st Amended Petition to Determine the Existence of Alfonso Portillo Irrevocable Trust, etc. and Amended First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc., are continued to May 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Joint Status Report to be filed by April 27, 2020.

Judge: Joeseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

Petitioner Elaine Jennings'S Petition: To Determine The Existence Of Alfonso Portillo Irrevocable Trust, Dated December 18, 2017; To Impose Constructi
Off Calendar Feb-03-2020 Continued To May-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Schreiber for and with Elaine Jennings.
Jennifer McGuire for Michael Linares (Respondent).

Ms. McGuire stated that the Settlor died on November 2nd, that there was a bit of a holt on things as her Client was grieving, that they have not gotten very far with discovery, that they do intend to respond and will likely do an amendment to the accounting and that they recently substituted in. Mr. Schreiber stated that the same thing ("recently substituted in") was said at the last hearing, that at the last hearing he requested suspension of the Trustee, that distributions were made to pay the remainder beneficiaries Attorney fees and asked that the objections be heard today. Mr. Schreiber once again asked that the Trustee be suspended, that the Court order that funds are not to be used, stated that all of the Successor Trustees have resigned and have admitted that they improperly used funds, and that there is a problem with the Trustee being very bias and siding with the other Beneficiaries against his Client. Mr. Schreiber also stated that the Court has the ability and should appoint a neutral Fiduciary to manage the estate. Ms. McGuire stated that she does not think there is a need to appoint a neutral Fiduciary, that the allegations are not accurate, that she prefers the opportunity to respond in writing, that the Beneficiaries asked her Client to pay those fees and that her Client acted on the advice of Counsel. The Court stated that to remove the Trustee would be a preliminary finding, that it will not do that, that removal requires an evidentiary hearing and that it will not do this piece meal. The amendment to the accounting and the reply are to be filed by January 2, 2020. The First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. and the First Amended Petition to Determine the Existence of Irrevocable Trust, etc. are continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial readiness. The Parties are to meet and confer and file a status report regarding discovery, what the outstanding issues are regarding the accounting, what the disputed facts are, the production of and obtaining the 2018 trust, whether the Parties are willing to move forward with ADR, the claims that are to be heard, the over time of the evidentiary hearing, the witness list and the time of the testimony needed for each witness. Mr. Schreiber asked for an earlier date on the issue of suspension and the Successor Trustee not using trust funds to defend this action. The Court informed Mr. Schreiber that he can file a motion to bifurcate.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Reporter: Elizabeth Wood, CSR #5134, 925-685-6222, EWood@ZandonellaZRS.com

Petitioner Elaine Jennings'S Petition: To Determine The Existence Of Alfonso Portillo Irrevocable Trust, Dated December 18, 2017; To Impose Constructi
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Continued To Dec-02-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeff Coons for Respondent/successor Trustee, Mike Linares
Michael Schreiber for and with Petitioner, Elaine Jennings.

Mr. Schreiber stated there is a conservatorship matter in Alameda County, that there is a lot of written discovery, the trust matter is a very much disputed case, both sides are going to want to do discovery, that Mike Linares is not paying rent while living on property owned by trust, there are multiple reasons for suspension of Mike Linares, and require the 2018 trust signed by Mr. Portillo, Sr. Mr. Coons stated they agreed to the conservatorship of estate, not of person, that they are expecting to prepare written discovery and depositions, request 90-120 days continuance, that they need to work with the account, they will file responses to notes to address these issues, and that he is not aware of any Feb. 2018 trust document. Mr. Schreiber stated the court is authorized to suspend the trustee for breach of fiduciary duties. The court stated it is not prepared to remove the trustee today, and instructed Mr. Coons to ask his client about the 2018 trust document and has 1 1/2 weeks to produce it. Ms. Schreiber stated Mike Linare's previous attorney admitted the 2018 trust documents exists. Mr. Coons request 30-45 days as he just substituted in as counsel in this case.

The 1) Petitioner Elaine Jennings's 1st Amended Petition to Determine the Existence of Alfonso Portillo Irrevocable Trust, etc., and 2) First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc., are continued to December 2, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status/surcharge/interim request for suspension and to oppose petition.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
